entries.text: The walls of this chamber recede forever. The map’s opposite edges connect to each other and Void spaces drop objects, players, and enemies on a random space somewhere else in the map.

An ancient summoning chamber from the Archon age. Place four altars somewhere in the map and mark them with Countdown:4, 5, 6, and 7. When any Countdown hits zero, convert each altar into a boss from a random faction. Altars can be disabled by interacting with them.

Effigies of Beelzebub line the sunken, furrowed walls of this arena. Enemies in this area do not have HP. To defeat them, they must collide with a wall. At the GM’s discretion, assign the following two effects to two minibosses in the current conflict, triggering when that miniboss is defeated: Place a D20 on the table turned to 1. At the end of every round, increase it by 1. Add the value of the D20 to all damage dealt to all parties in this conflict. Reduce the die by 1 if a creature enters AGNOSIA Each side places one Tower within Range:2 of their units, first the GM, then the Players. Both towers gain Countdown:4. Flip a coin, and assign one tower the result, and the other tower the opposite result. When Countdown hits zero, the Heads Tower heals 6 HP to all adjacent creatures, and grants any extra Healing as Temporary HP. The Tails Tower deals 6 damage to all adjacent creatures and inflicts either Poison:2 or Bleed:1. The towers swap their function at the end of this round and reset their Countdown.
A gargantuan statue has smashed through the ceiling and wrecked the structural integrity of this space. Each space in this area becomes Void after being stepped on twice.

Every surface in this chamber is covered in a pearlescent mirror-glass, shimmering visions of a Hell triumphant in them, with all the Archons and all the Humans buried in a crimsonblack prison. Select a Boss from a faction not actively in play. That boss appears in the center of the arena. At the start of every round the Boss changes to another boss from a random faction, retaining their appearance and HP.

A crystalline swamp where time flows in reverse. Huge glowing crystals grow from the muck and the air feels heavy. All players begin battle with Countdown:3. When countdown hits zero, each player rolls 3D4 and replaces their Speed with the total result. They regain Countdown:3 and continue their turn as usual.

A sentient chamber that twists and snarls, resisting the player at every step. Each player has a Burst:1 aura surrounding them. Every round, the GM may “shuffle” the battlefield once, redrawing the arena’s walls and floors except for the Burst:1 aura around the players.
A font in the center of the room gargles and sputters. Draw a 3x3 box around the center of the arena. Enemies can only die within this box.

The room is dotted with floating clusters of metal spikes. As an Aux Action, any character can Launch off any surface they are in contact with, travelling in a straight line until they collide with another object (a character can also be Shoved to move in this way—a smaller character always launches off a larger character, and characters of the same scale are launched off each other in opposite directions). Spiked clusters can also be shoved, dealing D8 damage when they collide with someone.
The room howls with fury as two ghostly armies clash above the shimmering red-blue spector of Oraios, the Archon of greed in a seemingly endless ceiling. All attacks in this arena gain Lingering:1.

The players begin TACCOM on separate one-square platforms in an ocean of Void. When they attack, their attack patterns create regular terrain over the Void. Enemy units are unaffected by Void in this room.

The air is filled with a Hyper-LSD variant. Deploy at least two small Swarms and a miniboss to begin. Every turn, spawn a new unit of your choosing, then flip a coin. On heads, it’s not real. It vanishes at the end of this round. On tails, it’s very real. As a Support Action, players may exchange places with each other at the end of their movement.

Physical rivers of time and reality flow through this roam, saturating the stone and metal surroundings with complex webs and eddies of fractalized time. Deploy at least one bosslevel unit and a supporting miniboss to begin. Every round, players must randomize who is controlling which character.

A plinth in the center of the room envelops the space in a headache inducing red-purple light. As long as the players are in this room, the roles of GM and Player are reversed. Select one player every round to control the enemy units, while the GM takes their character. Deploy a medium sized Swarm and three spawners to begin.

Causality is broken here—for every enemy in this conflict, the GM must mark a unique tile somewhere on the battle map. The enemy can only die on this tile. If the enemy receives enough damage to kill them on any other square of the map, they create a chronal instability. Remove the enemy and replace them with 1D6 Minions from any faction currently in play. These Minions can die anywhere.

The hylic-emulation here is unstable, loosened by a trillion subjective years of history pressing down at the edges. Get a 52-card poker deck, shuffle it, and make a deck with 10 cards drawn from the top. Begin TACCOM in a large room with 3 corridors, each leading off into a Fog Of War shadow. When players move down a corridor and enter a new room, draw a card. Reshuffle the deck and repeat the process whenever the players pass through an unexplored door. Rooms share their contents with every other room represented by that card—if you draw a Queen Of Diamonds card three times in a row, each room will be the same as the last. Enemies, objectives, and so on all remain the same as when the players left that room last.

The once-proud throne room of Iaos, Archon of Time. His shattered sun disc hangs low from the engraved ceiling. Four shards of it embedded in the floor shine a strange green-gold hue. These Shards are Obstacle:10 objects that cause massive instabilities when broken, converting a Burst:2 area centered around them into Void tiles. Here, time has become completely unmoored. Instead of standard Initiative, the GM begins each round by declaring what actions each of their units will take, players then take all their actions, before the GM plays out their predetermined moves in whatever order they desire. For each broken shard, the GM may spawn one miniboss-level unit from any active faction that takes its turn without this restriction, acting at the end of the round.

A mundane and empty chamber folds away, its walls shifting and vanishing like an impossible origami sculpture. The bright light of an alien world and the heat of its bioluminescent surface sear strange implications and half-grasped truths into the players minds. TACCOM takes place in a narrow corridor, no more than 4 or 5 squares wide. After 3 turns of combat, this corridor rises and begins to move forward. The players find themselves standing on the back or limb of a titanic beast. Create an Interactable space at the top of the corridor that allows the players to shunt the beast 2 squares left or right. Any attack with Push:2 or more will also move the beast a single space in the relevant direction. Other beasts maneuver beside, infront, or behind the player's mount, made up of whatever shapes or terrain effects the GM feels appropriate. Each contains a medium size Swarm, two minibosses, or a faction Boss. As the beast moves, it may move through pockets of Hazard terrain, which deal inflict either Slow:2, Bleed:2, or Poison:2 to anyone caught in them.

In the center of a very large room is a single Void tile with the Attractor effect over an infinite range. Once per turn, it emits an Arc:6 attack dealing 3 damage to any unit it touches, inflicting either Slow:2, Shaded:2, Healing:3, or Haste:1 (roll a D4 to determine which). If this attack kills a minion, that minion is converted to a Void tile with the Attractor effect over Range:2. The void tile in the center has all the traits of any Spawner tagged unit from any active enemy faction.

The chamber is in utter chaos already. Spectral psi-space imprints of a thousand battles rage before the players. In addition to a medium sized Swarm and one Faction Boss, create three Blob:4 clouds of ghostly mist around a large open area. Once per turn, spawn a unit from each Blob and reduce the Blob by that unit’s Scale. In this chamber, attacks gain the Delayed trait, their patterns only applying at the start of the attackers next turn. Attacks can harm allies caught in them.
